California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
April 4, 1990
Louise ElIiott
Keenan & Associates
Assistant vice President
5820 Shellmound St., Ste. 875
Emeryville, cA 94608
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-90-197
Dear Ms. ElLiott:
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Politica1 Reform Act (the rrActrt). r" Y"!r
iequest is one for general assistance on behalf of a third party'
acCordingly^ .,we are treating it as a request for informal
ass].stance. '/
OUESTIONS
When promutgating a conflict of interest code for the School
Employee aLnefiti Association, a Joint Powers Agency (the rrJPAr')
inltuiing a conmunity college district, 
should you be fo11ot'ring
the proc6clures for multi-county agencies as set out in Regulaticn
1875b.1, or instead, should you be following the procedures for
state agencies set forth in Regulation LA75o?
CONCLUSION
For purposes of the Act, a joint powers agency is a 'rlocal
gov"rnment agency'rt Thus, the JPA $'ou1d not folLow the procedures
ior state ag6ncies set forth in Regulation 18750. Whether the JPA
ir1- q".=ti"r, -would foIlow the procedures set forth in Regulation
L/ covernment code sections 8looo-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
corr"i==i"" regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations
Section 18000; et seq. iil 
references to regulations are to Titie
2, Division o'ot tne California code of Regulations'
2/ 
rnformal- assistance does not provide the requestor with the
im*rr"iiy provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
ico""."ir"'"t code seAtion i:rra; 
2 cal' code of Regs' section
1832e (c) ( 3 ) . )
428 j Street, Suite 8O0 . P.o. Box 8O? O sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322'366C


File No. I-90-■97
Page 2
18750.1 depends on s/hether its jurisdiction includes more than one
county.
fn accordance with Regulation 18750.1 (copy enclosed), a
joint powers agency whose jurisdiction 
encompasses more than one
county shall refer to the Fair Pol-itical Practices commission (the
rrCornrnissiontt) as its code reviewing body. As the code reviewing
body for nulti-county jurisdictional 
agencies, we require that the
joint powers aetency fo11ow the procedures set out in Regulation
18750.1 lrhen adopting or amending its conflict of interest code.
A joint powers agency whose jurisdiction rernains solely
reithin one county lrould refer to that county's board of
supervisors as its code reviewing body. Similar1y, a joint powers
agency whose jurisdiction is sole1y within one city would refer to
that city's city council as its code reviewing body' (Section
82011. )
FACTS
The School Ernployee Benefits Association, a newly forned JPA
in Stanislaus county has secured the services of your firm, Keenan
& Associates, to develop its conflict of j,nterest code as reguired
by Governnent code Section 87300.
Keenan & Associates proceeded with the developnent of the
written public notice and proposed code ag instructed by the
Cornnission during a conflict of interest code seminar held
september 26, L989, in San Francisco, and as set out in Regulation
18750.1.
Based upon the documentation you submitted with your letter,
we are unable to independently deternine the jurisdiction of the
JPA. However, based on past code packages prepared by Keenan &
Associates, and your recent attendance at a commission sponsored
seminar pertaining to the devel"opment of conflict of interest
codes, we feel confident in the accuracy of your deterrnination
that the JPA is in fact a multi-county jurisdictional 
agency.
ANALYSIS
For purposes of the Act, a rrlocal governnent agencyrr means a
county, city or district 
of anv kind includinq a school district,
or any other Local or regional political 
subdivision, or any
department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other
agency of the foregoing. (Section a2o41-, emphasis added.) Thus,
adrninistering agencies of a joint powers agency formed in
accordance with covernment code Section 6507 to benefit a
cornmunity college district 
are considered local government
agencies.
Regulation 18750.1 describes the procedures for the
promulgation and adoption of the conflict of interest codes for


File No. I-90-197
Page 3
local government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one
county. If the jurisdiction of this newly formed JPA is solely
within Stanislaus county, the Commj-ssion would not be the code
reviewing body. Instead, the JPA should be contacting that
county,s bo.ard of supervisors for instruction. 
If, however, the
JPA,s jurisdiction 
involves more than one county, you are correct
in referring to the commission as its code reviewing body.
It was mentioned in your lr{arch 9, 1990 letter that the
attorney for the Yosernite Cornrnunity College District believes that
the trproposed conflict of interest code and public notice do not
cornply with...the requirements for state entities found in the
Act, nor \./ith regulations propounded by the FPPC.!r we are aware
of recent legislation which may specifically designate cornmunity
colleges as rrstate agencj,estr. According to the office of the
chancellor of the California Conrnunity colleges, the effect of
this legislation on the status of community college districts 
as
state agenci.es has yet to be resolved.
Accordingly, it is premature for the Commissj'on to consider
lrhether this legislation might have changed the status of
connunity college districts 
and related joint powers agencies frorn
Iocal to state agencies for purposes of the Act.r/
I hope that I have answered your questions and concerns
regarding this natter. 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916) 322-5901 if you should have questior\s regarding the abcve
advice,
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
staff services AnaIYst
Legal Division
KED: cAH: 19 0197
Enclosures
3/ I,Ie note that a change in the classification of cornmunity
colLege districts 
for purposes of the Act arguably wouJ.d appear to
be an amendment to the definition of rrlocal government agencytr in
Section a2o4L- Any anendment to the Act must conply with Section
81012. we do not have sufficient infornation to deterrnine whether
the legislatj-on in question net the requirenents of Section 8101-2.
